feature_probes.description: Check whether FireFlow is enabled on the target appliance before spending deeper mapping effort there.
notes: Treat the activation script plus indicator file as the primary enablement check.
Use `/home/afa/.fa/config` `FireFlow_configured=yes` as supporting context, not as the only gate.
Treat this as a quick capability probe, not full proof of runtime health.
Use it early when choosing the next FireFlow mapping lab.
Current active downstream target profile.
The earlier `fireflow_enable` quick probe was too narrow and should not be treated as the decisive gate anymore.
The appliance-local activation script `/usr/share/fireflow/local/sbin/fireflow_activation.sh` returned `true` on the latest check.
The indicator file `/usr/share/fireflow/local/etc/site/fireflow_enabled` is present.
Supporting config evidence also shows `FireFlow_configured=yes` in `/home/afa/.fa/config`.
Candidate downstream target shared by the operator for later retargeting.
Use the same SSH credentials pattern as the current lab.
Probe `/home/afa/.fa/config` for `fireflow_enable` before deeper FireFlow mapping.
SSH key trust from `adf-dev` is now established.
The latest corrected probe reached the host and returned `fireflow_enable=missing`.
